channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: McConnell praises Israel’s 'unshakeable resolve' one year after Oct. 7 attacks
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) praised Israel’s “unshakeable resolve” to mark the first anniversary of the Oct. 7, 2023, attacks that left more than 1,100 people dead in Israeli communities outside of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party quits, a year before the national election
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A top official with German Chancellor Olaf Scholz's party announced his resignation on Monday, citing health problems, a year before an election in which the center-left Social Democrats face an uphill struggle, Kevin Kühnert, 35, has been the Social Democrats' general secretary — the
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ex-Philippine President Duterte to run as mayor despite his drug killings legacy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — The Philippines's former President Rodrigo Duterte registered Monday to run for mayor of his southern home city despite his notorious legacy over his brutal anti-drugs crackdown that the International Criminal Court is investigating as a possible crime against humanity.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: War rages on multiple fronts as Israel marks a year since Hamas' Oct. 7 attack
item.description: RE'IM, Israel (AP) — Israelis held somber ceremonies on Monday to mark a year since the deadliest attack in the country's history, a Hamas-led raid that shattered its sense of security and ignited wars on two fronts with no end in sight. Hamas marked the anniversary of its Oct.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas launches rockets from Gaza one year after Oct. 7 attacks, while IDF strikes terror targets
item.description: Hamas launched five rockets into Israel on the anniversary of the Oct. 7 terrorist attacks that started the war in Gaza, the Israel Defense Forces say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A Pakistani separatist group claims bombing that killed 2 Chinese near Karachi airport
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A Pakistani separatist group claimed responsibility for a late night bombing that targeted a convoy with Chinese nationals outside the country's largest airport, killing two workers from China and wounding eight people, officials and the insurgent group said Monday.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD and MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean woman sues government and adoption agency after her kidnapped daughter was sent abroad
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A 70-year-old South Korean woman sued her government, an adoption agency, and an orphanage Monday over the adoption of her daughter, who was sent to the United States in 1976, months after she was kidnapped at age 4.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Countries around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: Vigils, commemorations and acts of remembrance are being held across the world to mark a year since the Oct. 7 Hamas attack on Israel as leaders call for an end to antisemitism and the release of Israeli hostages.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian court sentences a 72-year-old American to nearly 7 years in prison for fighting in Ukraine
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — A Russian court on Monday sentenced a 72-year-old American in a closed trial to nearly seven years in prison for allegedly fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine strikes a Russian oil hub as Zelenskyy says the war is in 'a very important phase'
item.description: Ukraine’s military says it struck a major oil terminal in Crimea that provides fuel for Russia’s war effort.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The world's rivers faced the driest year in three decades in 2023, the UN weather agency says
item.description: The U.N. weather agency is reporting that 2023 was the driest year in more than three decades for the world’s rivers, as the record-hot year underpinned the drying up of water flows and contributed to prolonged droughts in many places.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senior member of Myanmar's former ruling party dies while serving prison sentence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Zaw Myint Maung, a senior member of Myanmar’s former ruling party arrested during the 2021 military takeover, died Monday while serving a prison sentence that was considered politically motivated. He was 72.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: American father of Hamas hostage Itay Chen pushes US, Israel on ‘Plan B’ as negotiations falter
item.description: The father of an American held by Hamas is urging the Biden administration to think outside of the box and return his son and others from Gaza as Israel's year-long strategy fails to free them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mozambique headed for crucial elections amid jihadist insurgency and drought-induced hunger
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambicans will vote this week for a new president who many hope will bring peace to an oil- and gas-rich northern province that has been ravaged by a jihadist insurgency for nearly seven years.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO and MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An explosion outside the Karachi, Pakistan, airport kills 2 workers from China and injures others
item.description: KARACHI, Pakistan (AP) — A massive blast outside the Karachi airport in southern Pakistan killed two workers from China on Sunday and injured at least eight others in the latest deadly attack on Chinese in the country.
item.dc:creator: ADIL JAWAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New film documents Oct. 7 attacks after first anniversary
item.description: Pierre Rehov, speaking from Ashdod, Israel, said he was disturbed by reactions that seemed to excuse the attack.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Sao Paulo mayor's race heads to a runoff without a controversial outsider in Brazil's elections
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Incumbent Ricardo Nunes and leftist lawmaker Guilherme Boulos will face each other in a runoff to decide the next mayor of Sao Paulo, following a first round of voting Sunday in Brazil’s local elections.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel-Hamas war will affect generations: Humanitarian worker
item.description: Since Israel's offensive began after the October 7 attacks, the death toll in Gaza is estimated at 41,800, according to the Palestinian Health Authority.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli crowds rally across the world on the eve of Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Crowds were participating in pro-Palestinian and pro-Israeli protests and memorial events across Europe, North Africa and Asia on Sunday on the eve of the first anniversary of the Hamas attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli official warns 'everything is on the table' as IDF prepares response to Iranian missile attack
item.description: Israeli Defense Minister Yoav Gallant told Fox News on Sunday that the IDF is preparing a response to Iran's missile attacks, and said that "everything is on the table.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Under fire: Israel's Iron Dome winning but pressure's on as conflict with Iran escalates
item.description: As the conflict in the Middle East intensifies, Iron Dome and Israel's other air defense systems have been continually challenged from prolonged use and evolving threats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia strikes Ukraine with drones and missiles
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces attacked Ukraine overnight with 87 Shahed drones and four different types of missiles, officials said Sunday. A 49-year-old man was killed in the Kharkiv region after his car was hit by a drone, said regional Gov. Oleh Syniehubov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel intensifies bombardment of northern Gaza, southern Beirut
item.description: Israel escalated its bombardment of northern Gaza and southern Beirut amid concerns about an intensifying conflict in the region with Iran and its military proxy groups, including Hezbollah and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Lebanese worldwide fear for their homeland and loved ones as violence escalates
item.description: It was a year ago when Jomana Siddiqui visited Lebanon, where her father was born — and is now buried. She planned to return there soon; this time, she thought, she would take her two teenage daughters. Instead, Siddiqui, who lives in California, now worries about relatives there.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: ROME (AP) — Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands of pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in major European cities and around the globe Saturday to call for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Macron calls for end to arms deliveries for use in Gaza
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron on Saturday called for a halt in deliveries of arms used in Gaza to Israel, adding that France was not sending arms to Israel.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Netanyahu calls Macron, other Western leaders who support arms embargo against Israel a ‘disgrace’
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu slammed Western leaders on Saturday who said they support an arms embargo on Israel over how its handling the Gaza war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rising from the ashes: Israelis in nation's war-torn south move home a year after October 7 massacre
item.description: The unique communal structure of the kibbutz plays a vital role in fostering resilience and support as people heal from the devastating impact of the October 7 massacre with many now returning home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Wildlife lover facing charges after allegedly assaulting passersby while attempting to capture a baby seagull
item.description: A Belfast woman was in court Tuesday after allegedly attacking passersby who tried to intervene with her capture of a baby seagull in August.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 teens detained following explosions near Denmark's Israeli Embassy
item.description: Two Swedish teenagers were detained Thursday on preliminary charges in connection with a pair of explosions near the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strike in Beirut killed 7 health and rescue workers
item.description: An Israeli strike overnight in the Lebanese capital Beirut killed seven health and rescue workers, an Islamic health organization said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: WikiLeaks' Julian Assange says he pleaded 'guilty to journalism' in order to be freed
item.description: STRASBOURG, France (AP) — WikiLeaks founder Julian Assange said Tuesday that he was freed after years of incarceration because he "pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: PASCAL BASTIEN, BARBARA SURK and SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea unveils its most powerful missile, which could reach North Korea's underground bunkers
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea unveiled its most powerful ballistic missile and other weapons that could target North Korea during a massive Armed Forces Day ceremony on Tuesday, as the South's president warned the North's regime would collapse if it attempts to use nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows Russian fighter jet flying within feet of US F-16 near Alaska
item.description: The release of the video comes after a series of Russian incursions into the Alaska Air Defense Identification Zone.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation
